issues.title: RFI table rows missing ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation
issues.description: The RFI list table lacks role='table', role='row', role='cell' attributes. Rows are not keyboard navigable (no tabIndex, no onKeyDown handler for Enter to open detail). Screen readers cannot parse the table structure. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA RFI criterion 14 requires tap-to-open on mobile (implies clickable rows), and Global Standards require keyboard navigability and axe-core compliance.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/RFIs.tsx, in the component that renders the RFI table/list: BEFORE: Table rows render as plain div or tr elements without ARIA roles, tabIndex, or keyboard event handlers. Clicking a row may navigate to detail but pressing Enter does not. AFTER: Add role='table' to the table container, role='row' to each row, role='cell' to each cell. Add tabIndex={0} to each data row. Add onKeyDown handler that calls the row click handler when Enter or Space is pressed. Add aria-label to each row like `RFI ${rfi.number} ${rfi.title} ${rfi.status}`. Ensure the currently focused row has a visible focus ring using outline: '2px solid #F47820' on :focus-visible. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Submittals page missing loading skeleton and error state
issues.description: The Submittals page shows a blank area or spinner while data loads instead of skeleton loaders matching the table layout. On fetch failure, there is no inline retry button or user-friendly error message. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require skeleton loaders matching final layout geometry within 100ms, and network failures must show toast + inline retry, never a full-page error.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Submittals.tsx, in the main Submittals component: BEFORE: While data is loading, the page shows either nothing, a generic spinner, or a brief flash of empty content. On error, the user sees no actionable recovery UI. AFTER: Add a loading state check. When isLoading is true, render a skeleton loader that matches the submittals table layout: 4-5 metric card skeletons at the top (matching the KPI bar dimensions), then 8-10 table row skeletons each 48px tall with pulsing animation (opacity oscillating between 0.3 and 0.7 over 1.5s). When there is an error, render an inline error banner with the message 'Unable to load submittals. Check your connection and try again.' and a 'Retry' button that calls refetch(). Do NOT show a full-page error. The skeleton and error components should use the same container width as the real table. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Change Orders page missing empty state with contextual CTA
issues.description: When there are zero change orders, the page shows a generic 'No data found' message or a blank table. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Change Orders criterion 10 requires: 'No change orders — scope is holding steady. When changes arise, document them here to keep the budget accurate.' with a 'Create Change Order' CTA. This violates Global Empty State rules which require contextual message + primary action button.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/ChangeOrders.tsx, in the main ChangeOrders component: BEFORE: When the change orders list is empty (data.length === 0 and not loading), the page renders a blank table, a generic 'No data found' text, or no meaningful guidance. AFTER: When data is loaded and the list is empty, render a centered empty state container with: (1) A FileText icon from lucide-react at 48px size in #9CA3AF color, (2) A heading 'No Change Orders Yet' in 18px 600 weight, (3) A paragraph 'Scope is holding steady. When changes arise, document them here to keep the budget accurate.' in 14px #6B7280, (4) A primary button 'Create Change Order' styled with background #F47820, white text, 8px border-radius, 40px height, that opens the create change order modal or navigates to the create form. Also add a separate empty state for filtered results: 'No change orders match your filters' with a 'Clear Filters' button. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Punch List table not responsive — horizontal overflow on mobile
issues.description: The punch list table does not collapse to card layout on mobile (< 768px). Columns overflow horizontally without frozen first column. Touch targets on status badges and action buttons are smaller than 44x44px. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require tables to collapse to card-based layouts on mobile or provide horizontal scroll with frozen first column. Punch List criterion 13 requires 'camera-first workflow' and swipe gestures on mobile.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/PunchList.tsx, in the component that renders the punch list table: BEFORE: The table renders the same multi-column layout on all screen sizes. On screens below 768px, content overflows horizontally. Status badges and action buttons have no minimum touch target sizing. AFTER: Add a responsive breakpoint check using a useMediaQuery hook or CSS media query for max-width 768px. When on mobile: (1) Replace the table with a card-based list where each punch item renders as a Card component showing: title (16px bold), location (14px muted), status badge (with colored dot), priority badge, assignee name, and due date. Each card should have min-height 72px and padding 16px. (2) Ensure all tappable elements (status badge, assignee, action buttons) have minimum 44x44px touch targets. (3) Add a CSS rule `@media (max-width: 768px)` that hides the table and shows the card list. On desktop (>768px), keep the existing table layout unchanged. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: RFI 'Ball In Court' indicator missing from list view
issues.description: The RFI list does not display a Ball In Court (BIC) column or indicator showing which party owns the next action. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA RFI criterion 4 requires: 'Ball In Court: shows the party name + colored dot. Clicking BIC opens the response thread filtered to that party's pending action.' BIC color coding is specified: GC (#3B82F6), Architect (#8B5CF6), Engineer (#14B8A6), Owner (#F47820), Sub (#6B7280). This is a core RFI workflow element that PMs check constantly.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/RFIs.tsx, in the component that renders the RFI table columns: BEFORE: The table columns include RFI number, title, status, priority, due date, and days open, but there is no Ball In Court (BIC) column showing which party currently owns the next action. AFTER: Add a 'Ball In Court' column to the table between Status and Due Date. For each RFI row, derive the BIC party from the assigned_to field or status: if status is 'open' and assigned_to exists, BIC is the assigned party; if status is 'under_review', BIC is the reviewer; if status is 'answered', BIC is the originator to accept/close. Render the BIC cell as: (1) A colored dot (8px circle) using these colors — GC: #3B82F6, Architect: #8B5CF6, Engineer: #14B8A6, Owner: #F47820, Subcontractor: #6B7280, (2) The party name text next to the dot in 14px font. If assigned_to is null, show 'Unassigned' in #9CA3AF italic. Add the column header 'Ball In Court' with the same 12px uppercase muted style as other headers. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's RFI module has robust Ball In Court tracking, email reply integration, and overdue escalation — SiteSync's RFI page currently lacks BIC visibility and email notifications, which are table-stakes features GCs expect. Fieldwire's punch list has a camera-first mobile flow with two-step verification that SiteSync's punch list does not yet match. Autodesk ACC's submittal workflow includes multi-party approval chains with enforced reviewer ordering — SiteSync's submittal page is missing this sequential approval enforcement. All three competitors have polished loading skeletons and contextual empty states across every page.
summary: The core-workflows module (RFIs, Submittals, Change Orders, Punch List) has functional list and detail views with API integration, but fails multiple PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requirements. The five most impactful issues are: (1) missing ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation on the RFI table, blocking accessibility compliance; (2) missing skeleton loaders and error recovery UI on Submittals, causing blank flashes and dead-end errors; (3) missing contextual empty states on Change Orders, showing generic 'no data' instead of construction-specific guidance with CTAs; (4) punch list table breaking on mobile with no card layout fallback, violating field-first principles; and (5) missing Ball In Court indicator on the RFI list, which is the single most checked column by PMs managing active RFIs. Overall score of 47 reflects a module that renders data but does not yet meet the polish, accessibility, responsiveness, or domain-depth standards required for production use by GCs.
